{"id":"smallhold-6bp","title":"'smallhold persona create': generate RSA-2048 keypair, insert account row","description":"Implement persona create: validate username ([a-z0-9_]{1,30}), generate RSA-2048 keypair, encode private key as PKCS8 PEM, public key as SPKI PEM, insert accounts row. The ap_id is constructed from config domain at creation time.

Agent workflow: Spawn a research subagent to determine which Rust RSA crate is most appropriate (rsa crate, rcgen) and which PEM encoding format Mastodon expects. Spawn a testing subagent to verify the generated keypair by signing and verifying a test message independently.

Test discipline:
- Verify generated PKCS8 PEM can be parsed back by the rsa crate
- Verify the SPKI PEM public key can be parsed independently
- Verify signing a test message with private key + verifying with public key works (using the rsa crate as oracle, NOT the same sign/verify functions we're testing)
- Test username validation: 'UPPER' is rejected, 'a-b' is rejected if hyphens not allowed, empty string rejected
- Test duplicate username: second create returns error with clear message

Defensive programming:
- RSA key generation: explicitly request 2048 bits — never let the default determine key size
- Never log the private key PEM, even at TRACE level
- The accounts.private_key_pem column: ensure DB permissions (file mode 600) in deployment docs","acceptance_criteria":"Generated PKCS8 PEM and SPKI PEM parse correctly with rsa crate; sign+verify roundtrip works; duplicate username returns clear error; private key never appears in logs","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"mark@wolfssl.com","created_at":"2026-04-23T00:27:35Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-04-23T00:27:35Z","metadata":{},"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"","depends_on_id":"smallhold-2eb","type":"relates-to","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:27:57Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","metadata":"{}"}]}
